source: Relief mechanics & figures from corpus/courses/prs-basics.md ('Tax-relief mechanics': up to RM3,000/year PRS contribution qualifies for personal income tax relief, scheduled through YA2030; RM250/month = RM3,000/year; '~RM700+ in real tax savings for a top-rate taxpayer'). Marginal-rate schedule: LHDN resident-individual income tax rates YA2024 (hasil.gov.my), cross-checked against RinggitPlus 'Malaysia Personal Income Tax Guide 2025 (YA 2024)'.
notes: PRS relief is a DEDUCTION from chargeable income (up to RM3,000/year), not an automatic cash refund. The real tax saved = RM3,000 x the taxpayer's MARGINAL rate (the rate on the top slice of their chargeable income). This dataset derives that saving at the four upper-middle marginal brackets of the LHDN YA2024 resident-individual schedule. Bracket boundaries (chargeable income, RM): 19% = 70,001-100,000; 24% = 100,001-250,000; 24.5% = 250,001-400,000; 25% = 400,001-600,000. RM3,000 x 24% = RM720, which backs the course's 'RM700+' top-rate claim. Saving assumes the full RM3,000 falls within a single marginal bracket and the taxpayer has at least RM3,000 of chargeable income taxed at that marginal rate (true for incomes comfortably inside each bracket). FLAG: relief amount and rate schedule must be re-verified against the active LHDN Year of Assessment before campaign use (relief scheduled through YA2030; rates may change at Budget). Lower brackets (1%-11%) save proportionally less and are out of scope for the 'RM700+' framing.
year_of_assessment: YA2024 (rate schedule); relief scheduled through YA2030

rows.note: Backs the course's 'RM700+' top-rate claim: RM3,000 x 24% = RM720.
